files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index c2916f5..20f47ee 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -90,2 +90,8 @@ Week 13 / day 84 observations:
 - Reverted B/C quotas to 25k/250k and capacity to tier 2, matching the last no-overload setup before the quota expansion. Cut ops to $2k global plus $1.5k S2 targeted.
 - Raised list prices to A=$9, B=$59, C=$149 to improve renewal cash if the high-quality S2 base tolerates it. Sent remaining E3 leads 7133/9859/9929 C-only offers at $39/seat.
+
+Week 14 / day 91 observations:
+- Cash fell only $10.9k to $42.5k, but the price hike caused 347 downgrades and active MRR fell to roughly $189k. Tier 2 caused 5.6% overload, no outage.
+- Next 7 billing mods imply about $56k of pre-churn subscription collections, so solvency depends on cutting compute/ops while avoiding another outage.
+- Reverted list prices to A=$7, B=$49, C=$119 to reduce further downgrade pressure. Lowered C model tier from 4 to 3 to cut compute; S2 C quality should remain high enough, but E3 close probability may weaken.
+- Moved back to capacity tier 3 for reliability, cut ops to $1.5k global plus $1k S2 targeted, kept ads/dev off, and sent final low E3 C-only offers at $29/seat.
